categories.name: Formal/Colloquial Substitution
categories.description: Model outputs colloquial Hindi words where reference uses formal equivalents

categories.name: Vowel Sign Confusion
categories.description: Incorrect matra (vowel diacritic) causing similar-sounding word errors

top_3_fixes.fix: Add a post-processing synonym normalization layer using a Hindi formal-informal synonym dictionary
top_3_fixes.implementation_hint: Build a dict mapping colloquial words to formal equivalents, apply after decoding

top_3_fixes.fix: Apply Unicode NFC normalization and add matra-confusable pairs as data augmentation during training
top_3_fixes.implementation_hint: Use unicodedata.normalize('NFC', text) on all training labels before tokenization

top_3_fixes.fix: Add a Sanskrit/Hindi sandhi-aware tokenizer as a pre/post-processing step
top_3_fixes.implementation_hint: Use indic-nlp-library's tokenizer to re-segment merged words in the hypothesis

fix_proposals.fix: Add a post-processing synonym normalization layer using a Hindi formal-informal synonym dictionary
fix_proposals.implementation_hint: Build a dict mapping colloquial words to formal equivalents, apply after decoding

fix_proposals.fix: Apply Unicode NFC normalization and add matra-confusable pairs as data augmentation during training
fix_proposals.implementation_hint: Use unicodedata.normalize('NFC', text) on all training labels before tokenization

fix_proposals.fix: Add a Sanskrit/Hindi sandhi-aware tokenizer as a pre/post-processing step
fix_proposals.implementation_hint: Use indic-nlp-library's tokenizer to re-segment merged words in the hypothesis

fix_results.implemented_fix: Unicode NFC normalization on hypothesis and reference strings
fix_results.fix_rationale: Devanagari text can represent the same visual character using multiple Unicode byte sequences (e.g., anusvara as composed NFC vs decomposed NFD). Whisper sometimes outputs NFD while references are NFC, causing WER penalties that are not real transcription errors.
